subject: Company Law
module_title: Introduction to Company Law and Incorporation
lesson_title: What is Company Law? The Nature of a Company

questions.question: Which Act consolidated Irish company law into a single statute?
questions.explanation: The Companies Act 2014 consolidated 16 separate company law statutes into a single Act of 1,448 sections, coming into operation on 1 June 2015.

questions.question: How many sections does the Companies Act 2014 contain?
questions.explanation: The Companies Act 2014 contains 1,448 sections, making it one of the most substantial pieces of legislation in Irish legal history.

questions.question: A company acquires legal personality upon the issue of which document?
questions.explanation: Under section 37 CA 2014, upon registration the Registrar issues a certificate of incorporation, at which point the company becomes a body corporate with a legal existence separate from its members.

questions.question: Under section 37 CA 2014, what is the effect of incorporation on the liability of members of a company limited by shares?

options.A: Members are personally liable for all company debts
options.C: Members' liability is limited to the amount unpaid on their shares

questions.explanation: Section 37 CA 2014 provides that incorporation limits members' liability to the amount, if any, unpaid on their shares. This is the foundational benefit of the limited liability company.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the five types of company that may be formed under the Companies Act 2014?
questions.explanation: The CA 2014 provides for five types of company: LTD, DAC, PLC, CLG (company limited by guarantee), and ULC. A registered partnership is not a company type under the CA 2014.

questions.question: In Salomon v Salomon & Co Ltd [1897], the House of Lords held that a company is:

options.A: An agent of its principal shareholders
options.B: A separate legal entity distinct from its members
options.C: An alter ego of the majority shareholder
options.D: A partnership in disguise if it has fewer than seven members

questions.explanation: In Salomon v Salomon & Co Ltd [1897] AC 22, the House of Lords definitively established that a validly incorporated company is a legal entity entirely separate and distinct from its members, regardless of who owns or controls it.

questions.question: Which body is responsible for the registration of companies in Ireland?

options.D: The Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment

questions.explanation: The Companies Registration Office (CRO) is the central repository of public statutory information on Irish companies and is responsible for registration of new companies and maintenance of the public register.

questions.question: Which of the following is a consequence of separate legal personality?

options.A: The company cannot own property in its own name
options.B: The company can sue and be sued in its own name
options.C: The company's assets belong to its shareholders
options.D: Shareholders are liable for the company's debts

questions.explanation: A consequence of separate legal personality is that the company can sue and be sued in its own name, enter contracts, own property, and employ staff — all distinct from any action taken by its shareholders.

questions.question: Perpetual succession means that a company:

options.A: Automatically dissolves on the death of its founding member
options.B: Continues in existence regardless of changes in membership
options.C: Must wind up after 25 years of trading
options.D: Passes to the heirs of the majority shareholder on death

questions.explanation: Perpetual succession is a key characteristic of incorporation: the company continues to exist regardless of changes in its membership through death, retirement, or transfer of shares.

questions.question: The document that governs a private limited company (LTD) under the CA 2014 is called:

options.A: The memorandum and articles of association

questions.explanation: Under the CA 2014, a private limited company (LTD) has a single constitutional document called the constitution, which replaces the separate memorandum and articles of association used under the older regime.
